2011 BMW 1 Series M ...  [8.36]
BIMMERPOST Web 2.0 Thread (Bugs, comments, suggestions, new requests)
Ok the time has arrived where we start looking at upgrading the forum software! So many people have rightfully been asking for changes here and the site is now in a position to act on those upgrades. Here is some background, I've been working on these backend upgrades for several years and they've been in place and running for as much as long. Notably you've seen them in action with the iOS App, but they are present here as well. If you noticed some performance improvements over the years, its because of those changes. Now it's time to take the App's full fledged backbone and put it in full place for the web version and today marks the first public preview of that!

This change opens up endless possibilities of what can be done, but it has been a complete re-write and re-thinking of everything so there are a lot of things that work and a lot more that does not work. The goal for this first preview is to fix everything that is broken and then add back in the things that are missing followed by adding in the new features.
  1. Fix what's broken (bugs)
  2. Add missing features
  3. Implement new ideas
If you feel inclined to try out the beta site and play around, use it find problems, give feedback on what works and what doesnt work it would be greatly appreciated

In the next post I'll setup a tracker post to help organize a TODO list for known issues or things being worked on so we don't clutter the thread with repeated comments on the same thing. But if you notice the tracker post doesnt fully describe a problem you are seeing by all means please make another post and let me know

As our first start we have implemented two of the BIMMERPOST sites, which are the original two sites so it is fitting to start fresh right where it all began:
https://www.e90post.com/beta
https://www.m3post.com/beta

Now aside from some visual touchups the big new things we have are a totally new search engine. The search engine is accessed from the top navigation bar. There will be an advanced search engine forthcoming, right now I am just playing with algorithms to see what works and what doesnt, so please feel free to try out the search engine and give feedback if you think the results are good/bad/otherwise. For sure advanced search that will give more results and fine tuned filtered will be introduced. Next there is an all new editor that gives you more control of how posts look and more true to what gets posted. It has many possibilities for us to add new elements.

And finally, as a general reminder this is a very early beta version so it's actively being worked on. You might suddenly see things break or change that because I'm essentially working on this place so you might be viewing the site during such a change
__________________
BMW 1M.
BMW M2.
BMW M2.
Last edited by apptest; 03-22-2026 at 01:39 PM..

BETA Active sites
  • https://www.e90post.com/beta
  • https://www.m3post.com/beta
  • https://g45.bimmerpost.com/beta
  • https://g50.bimmerpost.com/beta
  • https://g80.bimmerpost.com/beta
  • More sites forthcoming
BUG/TODO Tracker
  • COMPLETE [Search] - Advanced search and advanced filtering
  • [COLOR="rgb(119,187,65)"]COMPLETE [/COLOR][User profile] - View a users post / thread history
  • [Dark mode] - Expand dark mode use across all pages
  • [Mobile] - Limited/non-existent mobile design
  • [Email] - Send emails for all types of forum activities
  • [Email] - Control panel updates to add control over the activities/types of emails received
  • [Email] - UTF8 doesnt fully work, there is mangled mojibake
  • [COLOR="rgb(119,187,65)"]COMPLETE [/COLOR][Settings] - Avatars
  • [COLOR="rgb(119,187,65)"]COMPLETE[/COLOR] [Thread list] - Stickies not clickable
  • [Replies] - When reply is last on the page, the page should refresh to the next page where the post resides
  • [COLOR="rgb(119,187,65)"]COMPLETE [/COLOR][Editor] - Add tooltips to the icons?
  • [COLOR="rgb(119,187,65)"]COMPLETE [/COLOR][Editor] - Code block not fully correctly translating
  • [COLOR="rgb(119,187,65)"]COMPLETE[/COLOR] [Editor] - Strikethrough regression
  • [COLOR="rgb(119,187,65)"]COMPLETE [/COLOR][Sign out] - Icon shifts during sign out
  • [COLOR="rgb(119,187,65)"]COMPLETE [/COLOR][Postbit] - Show name of country on hover
  • [COLOR="rgb(119,187,65)"]COMPLETE [/COLOR][Editor] - Drafts
  • [Drafts] - Have a centralized place to view drafts
  • [Editor] - Drafts accessable from app (and vice-versa)
  • [COLOR="rgb(119, 187, 65)"]COMPLETE [/COLOR][Editor] - On long posts you have to scroll up to see the toolbar
  • [Posts] - Signatures
  • [Posts] - A way to link to specific post
  • [COLOR="rgb(119,187,65)"]COMPLETE [/COLOR][Thread list] - Goto last post icon (date)
  • [COLOR="rgb(119,187,65)"]COMPLETE [/COLOR][Threads] - Classifieds support
  • [Threads] - Polls support
  • [Attachments] - Get statistics such as views
  • [Member profile] - Logged in status?
  • [Member profile] - Garage list / link
New additions
  • Additional reactions (besides appreciates)
  • [General] - Locale support
  • [COLOR="rgb(119,187,65)"]COMPLETE [/COLOR][New posts] - Filter by MyForums

iTrader: (0)
Thanks for asking!

A bug I've experienced:

I continued to get emails for posts to subscribed threads even after turning off that option in settings. Went on for weeks. I ultimately unsubscribed cause I could find no other way of stopping the emails.

An enhancement I'd like to see:

Several Reaction options for posts, not just Appreciate. Appreciate is a good one, but sometimes you just want to laugh or say "wow" or express sadness.
Appreciate 1

It’s one that we won’t know for sure about until we go fully into the new software. Until then I will keep a watch on it. Ps let me know how dark mode treats you, good bad or otherwise 👍
Bug Observation II

Notifications - &quot;Mark all as read&quot;

After selecting function works to 'grey-out' posts; however, notification counter doesn't reflect zero after page reload.
Ok that part might be on purpose. &quot;hear me out&quot; 😁. The idea is that when it's highlighted in purple, that's showing you how many unread notifications you have. when its no longer highlighted (like what your screenshot has), then its a count of the total notifications you left in your box. You can test it out by marking one of those notifications as unread and refresh the page and it should go purple with a count of 1. try it out and see if it becomes more normal for you or if it still doesnt make sense after some time using it just let me know, none of this is set in stone, ill put it on the block for re-evaluation
Appreciate 2
smokinjoe643484.00
///M Power-Belgium89057.00

Quote:
Originally Posted by apptest
Ok that part might be on purpose. &quot;hear me out&quot; 😁. The idea is that when it's highlighted in purple, that's showing you how many unread notifications you have. when its no longer highlighted (like what your screenshot has), then its a count of the total notifications you left in your box. You can test it out by marking one of those notifications as unread and refresh the page and it should go purple with a count of 1. try it out and see if it becomes more normal for you or if it still doesnt make sense after some time using it just let me know, none of this is set in stone, ill put it on the block for re-evaluation
Understood...as suggested gave it a go.
On this occasion, outcome was as described - marked your last notification as unread after viewing, then refreshed.
&gt; Previous posts marked (4) unread disappeared as expected.
&gt; Reloaded page 'notification tally' then accurately illustrated (1) unread.
Lookin' good so-far...
Thinkin' once outta beta, updated UI instruction(s) likey needed to educate on 'how-to's' etc appropriate.

Appreciate responsiveness/replies to 'observations', etc

iTrader: (0)
Happy to see a thread like this. So many forums are being left to bit-rot.

Having performed a similar thing* in 2017/2018 to rescue my home forum, I'm happy to beta test and report things as I see them and make suggestions for improvements.

*Believe it or not we jumped ship from vBulletin back then and went with a completely custom solution written from scratch by yours truly. It's been a wild ride, but it has us in a better position today than we've ever been in the past. If you're morbidly curious you can read about it here: https://www.sr20-forum.com/server-ne...ges-ahead.html
__________________
BenFenner's 1994 Black SE-R (given away)

nice, excited to try it out.

my issue on the original set up is last year or so I would not consistently receive emails letting me know I have a message etc.. that needs attending to on the forums. So I have to constantly log in and check to make sure I didn't miss any messages. It would be nice if it was not so random and more consistent

Quote:
Originally Posted by GOLFFRR
nice, excited to try it out.

my issue on the original set up is last year or so I would not consistently receive emails letting me know I have a message etc.. that needs attending to on the forums. So I have to constantly log in and check to make sure I didn't miss any messages. It would be nice if it was not so random and more consistent
For sure that is one of the main goals/fixes for the new forum. Because the old software still wants to send emails there would at times be duplicate emails or conflicts so for the time being its disabled for the most part on the new forum (barring account settings changes and registration). Once we are able to move 100% to the new site then I can very easily round out the email situation and we should be good to go, I'll add it to the above Roadmap but just to be aware of it, but it will literally have to be the last part of the process. TBH ive already built and put the pieces in place so its mostly just turning on the switch 👍

And yes looking forward to having you and more ppl try it out and post up here about anything and everything good or bad I want to hear it and put in patches. so far the use has been good and i've already been able to put in so many little fixes here in there in the background as more and more people are using it
Last edited by apptest; 02-25-2026 at 11:40 AM..
Appreciate 3
GOLFFRR12429.00

iTrader: (4)
Quote:
Originally Posted by apptest
For sure that is one of the main goals/fixes for the new forum. Because the old software still wants to send emails there would at times be duplicate emails or conflicts so no emails. Once we are able to move 100% to the new site then I can very easily round out the email situation and we should be good to go, I'll add it to the above Roadmap but just to be aware it will literally have to be the last part of the process. TBH ive already built and put the pieces in place so its mostly just turning on the switch 👍

And yes looking forward to having more ppl try it out and post up here about anything and everything good or bad I want to hear it and put in patches. so far the use has been good and ive already been able to put in so many little fixes here in there in the background as more and more people are using it
awesome, I have no doubt you all will dial it in. Any other way I could help personally with anything just let me know. Cheers

bimmermookie

186
Rep
173
Posts

Drives: 25 M2 ZF; 16 M235i 6M RWD; E90
Join Date: Apr 2011
Location: northeast

iTrader: (0)
Maybe it's just me, but not seeing many recent posts
So... I regularly click on "New Posts" to check for updates to threads I'm following and anything else new that people are posting. But almost without fail, it will not show posts that I know are very recent -- more recent than posts it is showing.

I'm in the G87/G42 forum. I actually am interested only in G87 content, but this is what I get when selecting G87 from the forum list.

So I click on New Posts and threads that I know I or someone else posted to recently don't show up at all.

Here's just one recent example: there's this thread: https://g87.bimmerpost.com/forums/sh....php?t=2231960 The last post in it was by me, at 12:27 today.

But when I go to New Posts here: https://g87.bimmerpost.com/forums/se...chid=146202140 it is nowhere to be found. Even though this list of "new posts" includes ones made an hour or two before mine.

Is this a bug that can be fixed? Or is it my settings?

Also, is there a way to see only G87 content?

Thanks!

PS -- I re-up my suggestion for reactions other than Appreciate. Sometimes you want to Love, or Laugh, or say this is Sad, or Helpful.
Appreciate 1
apptest5543.00
Quote
bimmermookie
View Public Profile
Find More Posts by bimmermookie
      03-12-2026, 10:15 AM   #20

I'm in the G87/G42 forum. I actually am interested only in G87 content, but this is what I get when selecting G87 from the forum list.

So I click on New Posts and threads that I know I or someone else posted to recently don't show up at all.

Here's just one recent example: there's this thread: https://g87.bimmerpost.com/forums/sh....php?t=2231960 The last post in it was by me, at 12:27 today.

But when I go to New Posts here: https://g87.bimmerpost.com/forums/se...chid=146202140 it is nowhere to be found. Even though this list of "new posts" includes ones made an hour or two before mine.

Is this a bug that can be fixed? Or is it my settings?

Also, is there a way to see only G87 content?

Thanks!

PS -- I re-up my suggestion for reactions other than Appreciate. Sometimes you want to Love, or Laugh, or say this is Sad, or Helpful.
That new posts link is for our original site, not the new beta site. So I think these issues have to do with the old site? TBH g87 does not yet have a beta site implementation so im assuming it is. But check back here often as I will try to add G87 to the beta site list soon and you can see if you are having problems with new posts there

vreihen16

Join Date: Jun 2019
Location: Orange County, NY

iTrader: (0)
One quick config request, in case it is an option in the new forum software (which I assume is still vBulletin).

The biggest annoyance that another forum I used to be a member of that migrated to XenForo introduced is that all images in posts are presented in .webp format. When someone wants to save an image or drag/drop it from a web post onto a Mac desktop, the image is saved in a .webp file that can only be viewed in a web browser and can't be marked up or edited. If someone wants to save an image from a post, it requires either grabbing a screen shot or else using a clunky browser plugin to save that image to a .png file.

If the new forum software has this option, please...for the love of all things wearing roundels...do not enable .webp image format in the rendered message pages.....
__________________
Currently BMW-less.
Appreciate 0
Quote
vreihen16
View Public Profile
Find More Posts by vreihen16
      03-14-2026, 07:33 AM   #26
apptest
all I do is test!!6
5543
Rep
1,037
Posts

Drives: I lost count of how many2
Join Date: Aug 2010
Location: Akcent3

iTrader: (0)

Garage List
2027 bmw is great 👍  [10.00]
2025 tezting the app  [0.00]
Quote:
Originally Posted by vreihen16
One quick config request, in case it is an option in the new forum software (which I assume is still vBulletin).

The biggest annoyance that another forum I used to be a member of that migrated to XenForo introduced is that all images in posts are presented in .webp format. When someone wants to save an image or drag/drop it from a web post onto a Mac desktop, the image is saved in a .webp file that can only be viewed in a web browser and can't be marked up or edited. If someone wants to save an image from a post, it requires either grabbing a screen shot or else using a clunky browser plugin to save that image to a .png file.

If the new forum software has this option, please...for the love of all things wearing roundels...do not enable .webp image format in the rendered message pages.....
Webp is unavoidable since it offers a lot of performance benefits. We do use it alot all around the new site already (but not everywhere yet). It’s not really a forum thing but a general web use thing and I think it’s hard to find anywhere that doesn’t use it these days. The truth is there are some even newer formats that people are moving towards/getting wider spread adoption and we are as well, so believe it or not webp will be considered as an older not well used format in the future.

But to the core of the issue, you should absolutely be able to open web p images and manipulate them on a Mac. I’ve done it many times on all sorts of Mac’s and windows pcs. Is this a very old version of macOS?
Appreciate 1
vreihen1631049.50

Since I have this account it's always been like that. I have tried making multiple other accounts before 2.0 and was never able to login.
Is this a known problem? Any fix?

Quote:
Originally Posted by apptest
No it should not be since he has no special characters in his name
I assume that the password is encrypted in the database so you can't check it, and would not rule that out for having a bad character either entered by the user or as an artifact of the character encoding thing with the database that was discovered a few days ago.

Would it be worth asking @parked_comp to try changing his password to one that only contains letters and numbers using whatever system lets him in, to test if it fixes the problem everywhere?????
__________________
Currently BMW-less.

      03-14-2026, 04:14 PM   #36
analogue
Lieutenant
938
Rep
526
Posts

Drives: 1M, iX
Join Date: Apr 2005
Location: WI

iTrader: (1)
1) When editing a post, the action menu is not state aware, so it lets you select edit repeatedly, causing multiple edit controls to stack up. (see image)

2) If you click "Stickies" on a forum header, you get the filtered list, but none of the threads can be clicked.

Side note: I have code setup to update the Service Bulletin sticky threads for the various forums from a daily GitHub action. I'll start to look at how it works to create or update posts on a beta site, but let me know if there is any opportunity to use an API instead of emulating browser behaviors.
Attached Images
 
Appreciate 1
apptest5543.00
Quote
analogue
View Public Profile
Find More Posts by analogue
      03-14-2026, 07:59 PM   #37
apptest
all I do is test!!6
5543
Rep
1,037
Posts

Drives: I lost count of how many2
Join Date: Aug 2010
Location: Akcent3

iTrader: (0)

Garage List
2027 bmw is great 👍  [10.00]
2025 tezting the app  [0.00]
Quote:
Originally Posted by analogue
1) When editing a post, the action menu is not state aware, so it lets you select edit repeatedly, causing multiple edit controls to stack up. (see image)

2) If you click &quot;Stickies&quot; on a forum header, you get the filtered list, but none of the threads can be clicked.

Side note: I have code setup to update the Service Bulletin sticky threads for the various forums from a daily GitHub action. I'll start to look at how it works to create or update posts on a beta site, but let me know if there is any opportunity to use an API instead of emulating browser behaviors.
Awesome catch(s).
1) This should now hopefully be fixed, you may have to do a CTRL-F5 / refresh to get the fresh page running and see if it successfully prevents you from performing multiple edits the way you show

2) This is still being worked on to get the best solution, work-in-progress but coming

3) We do have an API which could be used for this, but the problem is that im still working on it with changes happening every few weeks so it would probably be more of a headache then help at this point. But I see the API going to a stable form once the site is out of beta. It also uses multiple keys and a type of CSRF protection so plus or minus on how much of a headache that also creates for you vs just doing what you are doing now. But if you want to use the API reach out once the site is fully out of beta and I can show you
Last edited by apptest; 03-14-2026 at 08:02 PM..
Appreciate 1
